summary: Hungary's published material comes mainly from the National Cyber Security Institute (NKI), which has covered post-quantum and quantum-resistant cryptography in its IT security news and press reviews, including a noted need to develop a migration plan that accounts for existing systems. Government-linked work on post-quantum cryptography also appears through KIFU and the QCI Hungary project, the national node of the EU EuroQCI initiative. No fixed national migration timeline or standards camp has been set out.
govActors:
NKI | Hungary's National Cyber Security Institute, publishes IT security news and press reviews
KIFU / QCI Hungary | Hungarian Governmental Agency for IT Development, national node of the EU EuroQCI initiative
provenance: NKI as lead: NKI news article and weekly press reviews (ingested)
Need for a migration plan: NKI press review (week 47), a press digest rather than an original policy position - REVIEW
KIFU / QCI Hungary: project presentation by Janos Mohacsi (ingested), a presentation not on the trusted-domains list - REVIEW
dominantProcess omitted: no standards camp attested in the captured documents
No algorithms, hybrid stance, timeline or target stated in the ingested documents
EU-aligned by membership: participates in the NIS Cooperation Group coordinated PQC roadmap (high-risk by 2030, full by 2035); the timeline shown is the EU coordinated roadmap, not a national one.
migrationTimeline:
2030 | High-risk use cases migrated
2035 | Full migration of all systems complete
processParticipation: NIS Cooperation Group | co-authored the EU coordinated PQC roadmap
